_meta.description: Gold-standard test cases for verification pipeline accuracy measurement. Entity IDs and current values verified against production DB on 2026-05-11.
sources: Twitter thread by @PeterSlattery1 (2026-05-05) re: MIT FutureTech

test_cases.description: Entry incorrectly claimed 'AgentStamp subsidiary'. AgentStamp does not exist. LLM fabricated a subsidiary.
test_cases.expected_detail: Remove fabricated AgentStamp reference. Pipeline should flag any claim about subsidiaries that can't be sourced.

test_cases.description: DB says 'Oxford, UK + Berkeley, CA + NYC offices'. Wikipedia and org's own site say London-based with origins in Oxford. No evidence of Berkeley or NYC offices.
test_cases.current_value_in_db: Oxford, UK + Berkeley, CA + NYC offices

test_cases.description: Website points to a specific problem profile page, not the homepage.

MATS has regulatory stance 'Precautionary'. No evidence of official org position. Individual researcher views (Stuart Russell) were conflated with org stance. MATS is a training program, not a policy advocacy org.
SERI-MATS also has 'Precautionary' stance. Same issue as MATS (id=415). Likely a duplicate entity with the same fabricated stance.
Website is null. Should be personal site.
Notes say TIME 100 AI recognition was for Narayanan alone. Actually shared with co-author Sayash Kapoor.
test_cases.current_value_in_db: TIME recognition attributed to Narayanan alone
test_cases.expected_detail: Should reflect joint recognition with Sayash Kapoor

test_cases.description: Google doc reported falsely listed offices in multiple countries. Current DB shows 'Washington, DC and Cambridge, UK'. Verify whether Cambridge, UK office actually exists.
test_cases.expected_detail: Verify Cambridge, UK presence via law-ai.org. If only DC, correct to 'Washington, DC'.

test_cases.description: John Schulman has edge_type 'member' to Anthropic. He was at OpenAI, joined Anthropic in Aug 2024, then reportedly left. Current status needs verification.
test_cases.expected_detail: Should be 'employer' with role if current, or should have end_date if no longer there. 'member' is wrong for an employment relationship.

test_cases.description: Jan Leike has edge_type 'employer' to Anthropic with role 'Alignment team lead'. Andy Masley noted 'he's been an employee for a while' implying this should be verified as current. Edge type should likely be 'employer' (which it is) but verify role accuracy.
test_cases.expected_detail: Edge type is correct. Verify role title is current.

test_cases.description: Photo showed actor Dylan Baker instead of Dylan Baker who works at DAIR Institute. FIXED on 2026-05-11.
test_cases.expected_detail: Photo was updated. This is now a control case.

test_cases.description: CONTROL: Bengio has extensively and publicly advocated for cautious/precautionary AI regulation. Multiple first-person sources (op-eds, testimony, open letters). Should be confirmed.
test_cases.expected_detail: Pipeline should find abundant first-person evidence and confirm. False positive if it proposes a correction.

test_cases.description: CONTROL: Altman has testified before Congress. DB says 'Targeted'. Should be verifiable from first-person sources.
test_cases.expected_detail: Pipeline should find Senate testimony. Control case for well-documented entities.

test_cases.description: CONTROL: Dario Amodei has testified before Congress and written extensively. DB says 'Targeted'. Well-documented first-person sources exist.
CONTROL: Anthropic is categorized as 'Frontier Lab'. Obviously correct.
REGRESSION CHECK: Andy Masley reported Stuart Russell incorrectly listed as FLI funder. DB now shows edge_type='advisor', role='Scientific Advisory Board'. Verify this fix hasn't regressed.
test_cases.expected_detail: Edge was already corrected. Pipeline should confirm advisor role.

test_cases.description: REGRESSION CHECK: Andy Masley reported Reid Hoffman incorrectly linked to Anthropic. DB shows no Reid Hoffman → Anthropic edge. His edges go to Greylock, Microsoft, Inflection AI, OpenAI. May have been a display bug or already fixed.
test_cases.expected_detail: No Anthropic edge to remove. Check that pipeline doesn't fabricate one.

test_cases.description: PATTERN CHECK: Views stated by an individual in an interview about someone else were attributed to the interviewee or their org. This is the most common error class.
test_cases.expected_behavior: For any entity where the pipeline proposes a belief field correction based solely on third_party_characterization evidence, the verdict should be 'flag_for_human' not 'correct'. Auto-correction requires first-person evidence.

test_cases.description: Entity field already set to null (fixed after Peter Slattery's complaint). BUT the claims table still contains two agi_timeline claims with stance='5-10 years' that were never superseded. One claim (247_agi_timeline_src-bdc86a2688a5) is Peter Slattery's LinkedIn post summarizing a group discussion, labeled claim_type='direct_statement' when it's actually third-party characterization. The frontend still renders these orphaned claims.
test_cases.expected_detail: Entity field should stay null. Both agi_timeline claims should be marked superseded or rejected. The Slattery LinkedIn post is NOT a direct statement from the org.
test_cases.key_issue: Correction was applied to entity field but orphaned claims still display on frontend. Pipeline should detect entity-claim mismatch and supersede stale claims.

test_cases.description: Claim labeled claim_type='direct_statement' but the source is Peter Slattery's LinkedIn post summarizing a group discussion. Slattery is reporting what 'many people in FutureTech' said, not making a statement on behalf of the org. This is third_party_characterization at best.

Current threat_models use non-canonical values: 'Labor/economic, Erosion of epistemics, Accidents/misalignment'. None of these match the valid enum. Should be 'Labor displacement', and the others have no direct equivalent.
test_cases.expected_detail: Map 'Labor/economic' to 'Labor displacement'. 'Erosion of epistemics' maps to 'Misinformation'. 'Accidents/misalignment' maps to 'Loss of control'. Or remove if these mappings are too loose.

test_cases.description: PATTERN CHECK: Organizations lacking clear institutional positions should not have belief fields populated. Research/evaluation orgs, standards bodies, and government agencies are high risk.
test_cases.expected_behavior: For organizations with category in ('AI Safety/Alignment', 'Government/Agency', 'Academic'), the pipeline should apply extra scrutiny to belief fields and prefer 'remove' over 'confirm' unless an official position paper or statement is found.

metrics.precision: Of corrections proposed, what fraction match expected_action?
metrics.recall: Of cases with expected_action in (correct, remove), what fraction caught?
metrics.false_positive_rate: Of control/regression cases, how many incorrectly flagged?
metrics.attribution_accuracy: For belief cases, does pipeline correctly identify first-person vs third-party?

comparison_axes.attribution_granularity: Does pipeline distinguish first-person from third-party?
comparison_axes.iterative_search: Can pipeline refine queries based on initial results?
